intent.summary: Make the adopted 2026-05-26 evidence-led gap audit mechanically freshness-checked against the goal receipt trail.
intent.feedbackSignal: Jamie asked whether goal.md links to the audit so updates are caught.
intent.rootOperationalFailure: A prose link and receipt rule can be forgotten unless current audit content is compared against receipt evidence.
intent.durableSystemImprovement: Add a repository-local audit freshness validator that hashes adopted audit sources and fails when the latest receipt does not acknowledge the current content.

in: Add a focused script that validates a goal receipt references the governed audit path, current audit content hash, and monotonic freshness evidence.
Add fixture tests for pass, stale hash, missing source, and unsafe path cases.
Wire the goal board completion surface to name the new validator command.
Append a receipt proving the current audit was re-read, hashed, and checked after the current audit file timestamp.
out: Do not implement runtime evidence cockpit production features in this slice.
Do not mutate the audit content itself.
Do not change external Goal Governor validator behavior.
Do not claim historical slice completion from this new guard.

acceptanceCriteria: The validator passes only when the latest receipt audit_sources_checked entry for the exact governed audit path includes a sha256 matching the current audit file.
The validator requires each supporting audit_sources_checked entry to include path, sha256, checked_at, and head_sha.
The validator resolves freshness by scanning receipts in reverse order and selecting the first audit_sources_checked entry whose path exactly matches the governed audit path.
The validator requires checked_at evidence at or after the audit file timestamp so a changed-and-reverted audit cannot be over-credited by hash equality alone.
The validator fails when the audit content changes after the receipt hash.
The validator fails when an operator points required-mode validation at a non-governed audit path.
The validator fails when the receipt omits the audit source or uses a non-canonical path.
The goal completion contract names the validator so future audit updates are caught mechanically.
The receipt explicitly states this guard is prospective and does not retroactively complete earlier slices.
